originContent: A hot take on Lisp metaprogramming, `Autology` is a Lisp with access
translatedContent: A hot take on Lisp metaprogramming, `Autology` is a Lisp with access

translatedContent: I gave a talk about this project with London Clojurians (2025-05-13):

translatedContent: I also gave a shorter version of the same talk at Lmabda Days in Krakow (2025-06-13):

originContent: [Autology: Writing a self-rewriting Lisp - Lambda Days (YouTube)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-cppTsLgVbI)
translatedContent: [Autology: Writing a self-rewriting Lisp - Lambda Days (YouTube)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-cppTsLgVbI)

originContent: Autology is a functional interpreted Lisp language, written in Clojure.
translatedContent: Autology is a functional interpreted Lisp language, written in Clojure.

originContent: The Autology interpreter function is defined as a data structure which is available to the Autology program, it is bound to the variable `*i*`.
translatedContent: The Autology interpreter function is defined as a data structure which is available to the Autology program, it is bound to the variable `*i*`.

originContent: Whenever an expression needs to be evaluated, Autology will retrieve the current value of `*i*` from the current lexically scoped execution environment and use it to evaluate the expression.
translatedContent: Whenever an expression needs to be evaluated, Autology will retrieve the current value of `*i*` from the current lexically scoped execution environment and use it to evaluate the expression.

originContent: By binding new values of `*i*` we can modify the behaviour of the Autology language while our program is running.
translatedContent: By binding new values of `*i*` we can modify the behaviour of the Autology language while our program is running.

originContent: Mainly for fun and to explore a neat idea.
translatedContent: Mainly for fun and to explore a neat idea.

originContent: Autology is not a particularly useful language as-is, its very slow and resource intensive.
translatedContent: Autology is not a particularly useful language as-is, its very slow and resource intensive.

originContent: Macros are the normal tool for Lisp languages to modify their syntax or behaviour, but these are normally only available at compile time. Autology allows dynamic rewriting of the language at runtime.
translatedContent: Macros are the normal tool for Lisp languages to modify their syntax or behaviour, but these are normally only available at compile time. Autology allows dynamic rewriting of the language at runtime.

originContent: Even other non-Lisp languages are able to define some form of Domain Specific Language to allow programmers to express problems more clearly, however these are generally quite restrictive in scope. Autology allows full ad-hoc re-writing of the syntax of the language, as well as defining new features or removing existing ones.
translatedContent: Even other non-Lisp languages are able to define some form of Domain Specific Language to allow programmers to express problems more clearly, however these are generally quite restrictive in scope. Autology allows full ad-hoc re-writing of the syntax of the language, as well as defining new features or removing existing ones.

originContent: # What can you do with it?
translatedContent: # What can you do with it?

originContent: Well to start with you might like to add functions to the language. Autology doesn't have them by default, but we can rebind the `*i*` symbol to a data structure representing a new interpreter function, one that does have functions.
translatedContent: Well to start with you might like to add functions to the language. Autology doesn't have them by default, but we can rebind the `*i*` symbol to a data structure representing a new interpreter function, one that does have functions.

originContent: - Why not switch form applicative order evaluation to normal order evaluation?
translatedContent: - Why not switch from applicative order evaluation to normal order evaluation?

originContent: - Maybe modify the language so it uses a continuation passing style?
translatedContent: - Maybe modify the language so it uses a continuation passing style?

originContent: - Define a metacircular Lisp inside your program which has access to it's own interpreter as well at the Autology interpreter.
translatedContent: - Define a metacircular Lisp inside your program which has access to its own interpreter as well as the Autology interpreter.
